title: Beyond Mind and Speech
summary: Realizing the reality which cannot be described in words. Addresses communication breakdown, feeling misunderstood, and silence.

sections.summary: Themes: action and duty, devotion, mind and meditation. The passage moves from “As the scriptures say in many ways, the manifested universe is like a mirage. If everything is one eternal Consciousness, how can there be…” through “There is no distinction between life and death. There is no distinction between action and inaction. If everything is one eternal Consciousness,…” through “It is free of sadness and joy it is free of pride and humility. If everything is one eternal Consciousness, how can there be attachments and…”

Themes: action and duty, devotion, freedom from attachment. The passage moves from “Detachments and attachments do not arise in it. Greed and contentment do not arise in it. If everything is one eternal Consciousness, how can…” through “There is no difference between imagination and bodies. There is no difference between imagination and worlds. It is ultimate truth that I am…” through “Where there is no knowledge, verses do not go there. I am involved in the world and I am renounced. I have spoken the ultimate truth.”

verses.sanskrit: बहुधा श्रुतयः प्रवदन्ति वयं वियदादिरिदं मृगतोयसमम्। यदि चैकनिरन्तरसर्वशिव मुपमेयमथोह्युपमा च कथम्।।१।।
verses.translation: As the scriptures say in many ways, the manifested universe is like a mirage. If everything is one eternal Consciousness, how can there be comparison and compared?

verses.sanskrit: अविभक्तिविभक्तिविहीनपरं ननु कार्यविकार्यविहीनपरम्। यदि चैकनिरन्तरसर्वशिवं यजनं च कथं तपनं च कथम्।।२।।
verses.translation: Ultimately it is free from divisions and non-divisions, ultimately it is free from activity and rest. If everything is one eternal Consciousness, how can there be worship and practice?

verses.sanskrit: मन एव निरन्तरसर्वगतं ह्यविशालविशालविहीनपरम्। मन एव निरन्तरसर्वशिवं मनसापि कथं वचसा च कथम्।।३।।
verses.translation: It is the mind that is eternally omnipresent. Ultimately it is not big not small. Mind is nothing but eternal Consciousness. How can it be expressed via thought and speech?

verses.sanskrit: दिनरात्रिविभेदनिराकरण मुदितानुदितस्य निराकरणम्। यदि चैकनिरन्तरसर्वशिवं रविचन्द्रमसौ ज्वलनश्च कथम्।।४।।
verses.translation: It is without the distinction of day and night. It is without the distinction of rising and setting. If everything is one eternal Consciousness, how can there be Sun, moon and fire?

verses.sanskrit: गतकामविकामविभेद इति गतचेष्टविचेष्टविभेद इति। यदि चैकनिरन्तरसर्वशिवं बहिरन्तरभिन्नमतिश्च कथम्।।५।।
verses.translation: It is beyond the difference of desire and satisfaction. It is beyond the difference of effort and non-effort. If everything is one eternal Consciousness, how can there be differences of internal or external?

verses.sanskrit: यदि सारविसारविहीन इति यदि शून्यविशून्यविहीन इति। यदि चैकनिरन्तरसर्वशिवं प्रथमं च कथं चरमं च कथम्।।६।।
verses.translation: If it is devoid of essence and details, if it is devoid of nothing and something, if everything is one eternal Consciousness, how can there be a beginning and an end?

verses.sanskrit: यदि भेदविभेदनिराकरणं यदि वेदकवेद्यनिराकरणम्। यदि चैकनिरन्तरसर्वशिवं तृतीयं च कथं तुरीयं च कथम्।।७।।
verses.translation: If there is no distinction of difference and similarity, if there is no distinction of knower and the known, if everything is one eternal Consciousness, how can there be the third or the forth?

verses.sanskrit: गदितागदितं न हि सत्यमिति विदिताविदितं न हि सत्यमिति। यदि चैकनिरन्तरसर्वशिवं विषयेन्द्रियबुद्धिमनांसि कथम्।।८।।
verses.translation: The chanted one and the untold are not the truth. The known and unknown are not the truth. If everything is one eternal Consciousness, how can there be objects, senses, intellect and mind?

verses.sanskrit: गगनं पवनो न हि सत्यमिति धरणी दहनो न हि सत्यमिति। यदि चैकनिरन्तरसर्वशिवं जलदश्च कथं सलिलं च कथम्।।९।।
verses.translation: Sky and air are not the truth. Earth and fire are not the truth. If everything is one eternal Consciousness, how can there be clouds or water?

verses.sanskrit: यदि कल्पितलोकनिराकरणं यदि कल्पितदेवनिराकरणम्। यदि चैकनिरन्तरसर्वशिवं गुणदोषविचारमतिश्च कथम्।।१०।।
verses.translation: If there is no distinction between imagination and the world. If there is no distinction between imagination and the gods. If everything is one eternal Consciousness, how can there be discrimination of qualities and defects?

verses.sanskrit: मरणामरणं हि निराकरणं करणाकरणं हि निराकरणम्। यदि चैकनिरन्तरसर्वशिवं गमनागमनं हि कथं वदति।।११।।
verses.translation: There is no distinction between life and death. There is no distinction between action and inaction. If everything is one eternal Consciousness, how can there be transmigration?

verses.sanskrit: प्रकृतिः पुरुषो न हि भेद इति न हि कारणकार्यविभेद इति। यदि चैकनिरन्तरसर्वशिवं पुरुषापुरुषं च कथं वदति।।१२।।
verses.translation: There is no difference between Purusha and the Prakriti. There is no difference between cause and effect. If everything is one eternal Consciousness, how can I say about Purusha and not-Purusha?

verses.sanskrit: तृतीयां न हि दुःखसमागमनं न गुणाद्द्वितीयस्य समागमनम्। यदि चैकनिरन्तरसर्वशिवं स्थविरश्च युवा च शिशुश्च कथम्।।१३।।
verses.translation: Suffering of the third kind does not arise. Quality of the second kind does not arise. If everything is one eternal Consciousness, how can there be old age, youth and infancy?

verses.sanskrit: ननु आश्रमवर्णविहीनपरं ननु कारणकर्तृविहीनपरम्। यदि चैकनिरन्तरसर्वशिव मविनष्टविनष्टमतिश्च कथम्।।१४।।
verses.translation: In it there is no castes or stages of life. In it there is no cause or doer ultimately. If everything is one eternal Consciousness, how can there be discrimination between destructible and indestructible?

verses.sanskrit: ग्रसिताग्रसितं च वितथ्यमिति जनिताजनितं च वितथ्यमिति। यदि चैकनिन्तरसर्वशिव मविनाशि विनाशि कथं हि भवेत्।।१५।।
verses.translation: Swallowed and un-swallowed are both false. Born and unborn are both false. If everything is one eternal Consciousness, then how can there be destruction and creation?

verses.sanskrit: पुरुषापुरुषस्य विनष्टमिति वनितावनितस्य विनष्टमिति। यदि चैकनिरन्तरसर्वशिव मविनोदविनोदमतिश्च कथम्।।१६।।
verses.translation: Masculine and non-masculine are not present in it. Feminine and non-feminine are not present in it. If everything is one eternal Consciousness, how can there be discrimination of joy and sorrow?

verses.sanskrit: यदि मोहविषादविहीनपरो यदि संशयशोकविहीनपरः। यदि चैकनिरन्तरसर्वशिव महमेति ममेति कथं च पुनः।।१७।।
verses.translation: If it is ultimately devoid of attachment and misery, if it is ultimately devoid of doubt and grief, if everything is one eternal Consciousness, how can there be I and mine?

verses.sanskrit: ननु धर्मविधर्मविनाश इति ननु बन्धविबन्धविनाश इति। यदि चैकनिन्तरसर्वशिव मिहदुःखविदुःखमतिश्च कथम्।।१८।।
verses.translation: It is not a destroyer of right conduct or wrong conduct. It is not a destroyer of bondage and non bondage. If everything is one eternal Consciousness, how can there be suffering or happiness?

verses.sanskrit: हि याज्ञिकयज्ञविभाग इति न हुताशनवस्तुविभाग इति। यदि चैकनिरन्तरसर्वशिवं वद कर्मफलानि भवन्ति कथम्।।१९।।
verses.translation: There is no distinction of rituals of sacrifice and conductors of rituals. There is no distinction of sacrificial fire and offerings into the fire. If everything is one eternal Consciousness, how can there be consequences of actions?

verses.sanskrit: ननु शोकविशोकविमुक्त इति ननु दर्पविदर्पविमुक्त इति। यदि चैकनिरन्तरसर्वशिवं ननु रागविरागमतिश्च कथम्।।२०।।
verses.translation: It is free of sadness and joy it is free of pride and humility. If everything is one eternal Consciousness, how can there be attachments and detachments?

verses.sanskrit: न हि मोहविमोहविकार इति न हि लोभविलोभविकार इति। यदि चैकनिरन्तरसर्वशिवं ह्यविवेकविवेकमतिश्च कथम्।।२१।।
verses.translation: Detachments and attachments do not arise in it. Greed and contentment do not arise in it. If everything is one eternal Consciousness, how can there be discrimination and indiscrimination?

verses.sanskrit: त्वमहं न हि हन्त कदाचिदपि कुलजातिविचारमसत्यमिति। अहमेव शिवः परमार्थ इति अभिवादनमत्र करोमि कथम्।।२२।।
verses.translation: You and I were never existed. Family and caste are not true. It is the ultimate truth that I am Consciousness. Whom can I worship?

verses.sanskrit: गुरुशिष्यविचारविशीर्ण इति उपदेशविचारविशीर्ण इति। अहमेव शिवः परमार्थ इति अभिवादनमत्र करोमि कथम्।।२३।।
verses.translation: In it, the distinction of teacher and student vanishes. The teaching also vanishes. It is ultimate truth that I am Consciousness, whom can I worship?

verses.sanskrit: न हि कल्पितदेहविभाग इति न हि कल्पितलोकविभाग इति। अहमेव शिवः परमार्थ इति अभिवादनमत्र करोमि कथम्।।२४।।
verses.translation: There is no difference between imagination and bodies. There is no difference between imagination and worlds. It is ultimate truth that I am Consciousness, whom can I worship?

verses.sanskrit: सरजो विरजो न कदाचिदपि ननु निर्मलनिश्चलशुद्ध इति। अहमेव शिवः परमार्थ इति अभिवादनमत्र करोमि कथम्।।२५।।
verses.translation: I never was with action and without action. I am pure innocent and spotless. It is ultimate truth that I am Consciousness, whom can I worship?

verses.sanskrit: न हि देहविदेहविकल्प इति अनृतं चरितं न हि सत्यमिति। अहमेव शिवः परमार्थ इति अभिवादनमत्र करोमि कथम्।।२६।।
verses.translation: Distinction of embodied and disembodied is not there. Immoral and moral are not true. It is ultimate truth that I am Consciousness, whom can I worship?

verses.sanskrit: विन्दति विन्दति न हि न हि यत्र छन्दोलक्षणं न हि न हि तत्र। समरसमग्नो भावितपूतः प्रलपति तत्त्वं परमवधूतः।।२७।।
verses.translation: Where there is no knowledge, verses do not go there. I am involved in the world and I am renounced. I have spoken the ultimate truth.
